rag_rules: When fixing type mismatches in AGREE contracts, ensure left and right sides of binary expressions have identical types.
Only use assign statements for existing output features or declared auxiliary variables in implementation scope.
generic_rules: Validate that all AGREE expressions use only visible identifiers and declared helpers from the same annex.
Ensure AGREE scalar type declarations use int, real, or bool; avoid package-qualified or invalid type names.
Align helper declaration types with the types of their assigned expressions, especially for comparisons and arithmetic.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in annex agree {** ... **}; blocks with proper scope for component types vs implementations.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers in generated AGREE code unless explicitly present in the AADL model.
anti_patterns: Avoid adding new architecture elements to fix AGREE diagnostics; instead, correct type mismatches or invalid references in existing constructs.
Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scope; use only assign for output bindings.
